authors: Uthman OA, Sambala EZ, et al.
title: Systematic review of social determinants of childhood immunisation in LMICs and equity impact analysis in Nigeria
key_determinants: Wealth (richest vs poorest AOR 3.21; 95% CI 2.06-5.00), Maternal education (secondary+ vs none AOR 1.79; 1.39-2.31), ANC 4+ visits (AOR 2.87; 2.21-3.72), Religion, Ethnicity, Maternal age, Birth setting
notes: DTP3 coverage ~35%; Concentration indices: education 0.48, ANC 0.39, wealth 0.31

title: Zero-dose under-immunized and dropout children in Nigeria: Trend and contributing factors
key_determinants: Mother's education (tertiary vs none: -0.256), Household wealth (richest vs poorest: -0.065), Child age (-0.052)
notes: Substantial decline from 46% to 28% over 15 years; rural areas consistently higher

title: Individual and community-level determinants of pentavalent vaccination dropouts in SSA: multilevel analysis
key_determinants: Maternal age 15-24 (AOR 1.61; 1.51-1.72), Home delivery (AOR 1.65; 1.57-1.73), No ANC (AOR 1.34; 1.23-1.45), No formal education (AOR 1.30; 1.22-1.40), Poor wealth (AOR 1.09; 1.04-1.15)
notes: Western SSA highest dropout; community variance 1.11%; individual factors dominate

title: Widening geographical inequities in DTP vaccination coverage and zero-dose prevalence across Nigeria (2018-2024)
key_determinants: Geopolitical zone, Urban/rural residence, Wealth, Education
notes: North-West and North-East zones have lowest coverage and highest dropout

title: Factors influencing childhood immunisation uptake in Africa: systematic review
key_determinants: Maternal education, Household wealth, ANC attendance, Place of delivery, Distance to facility, Media exposure, Birth order
notes: Systematic review confirming consistent determinants across SSA

title: Immunization coverage and determinants among children 12-23 months in East Africa: Bayesian hierarchical model
notes: 21% country-level variance; 32% community-level variance in unimmunised status

title: Determinants of defaulting from completion of child immunisation: Laelay Adiabo District Ethiopia
key_determinants: Distance, Maternal knowledge, Health facility factors
notes: Case-control design; facility access key barrier

title: Determinants of uptake of third doses of OPV and DTP in Ibadan Nigeria
dropout_prevalence: 71% completion at tertiary; 31.6% at secondary; 35.1% at primary facility
notes: Facility type strongly predicts dose completion
